Block.Para: An Act to amend the Broadcasting Act and the Income Tax Act (closed-captioned programming)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 39th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the First Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Broadcasting Act to require broadcasters to provide closed captions for their video programming. It also amends the Income Tax Act to allow a tax deduction for broadcasters for the purchase of closed-captioning technology.
An Act to amend the Broadcasting Act and the Income Tax Act (closed-captioned programming)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. The Broadcasting Act is amended by adding the following after section 3:

Para.change: 3.1 Each broadcasting undertaking shall broadcast its video programming with closed captions.

Block.Para: 2. Subsection 20(1) of the Income Tax Act is amended by striking out the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (vv), by adding the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (ww) and by adding the following after paragraph (ww):

Block.MarginalNote: Expenses relating to the broadcast of closed-captioned video programming

Para.change: (xx) where the taxpayer is the holder of a licence to carry on a broadcasting undertak-ing or is exempt from the requirement to hold a licence under the Broadcasting Act, an amount not otherwise deductible paid by the taxpayer in the year for expenses relating to the closed-captioning of video programming.

Block.Para:
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
